data.title: PNC Financial Q1 earnings beat, revenue misses amid market uncertainty
data.description:
PNC Financial Services (NYSE:PNC) delivered a mixed Q1, with earnings beating the Wall Street consensus, while revenue missed. Its net interest income topped the consensus, and its net interest margin improved from both the prior quarter and a year ago. However, its provision for credit losses rose as the company braces for an uncertain environment.

The bank expects reaffirmed full-year 2025 guidance of net interest income up 6%-7% Y/Y, noninterest income up ~5%, total revenue up ~1%, and stable average loans.

The company expects Q2 net interest income to rise 1%-2% from Q1's $3.48, implying $3.54B-$3.55B (vs. Visible Alpha consensus of $3.55B). Total revenue is expected to increase 1%-3% from Q1's $5.45B, implying $5.51B-$5.62B vs. the average analyst estimate of $5.64B.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $3.51, topping the average analyst estimate of $3.38, fell from $3.77 in Q4 2024 and climbed from $3.10 in Q1 2024.

Q1 revenue of $5.45B, missing the $5.48B consensus, dropped from $5.57B in the previous quarter and increased from $5.15B in the year-ago period.

"We grew customers and commercial loans, expanded our net interest margin, increased capital levels and maintained solid credit quality metrics," said Chairman and CEO Bill Demchak. "While market uncertainty impacted our capital markets activity, expenses remained well-controlled, resulting in another quarter of strong results."

Net interest income dipped 1% Q/Q and increased 6% Y/Y to $3.48B, beating the Visible Alpha estimate of $3.44B. Non-GAAP net interest margin increased to 2.78% from 2.75% in the previous quarter and 2.57% a year ago.

Noninterest income was $1.98B, down from $2.04B in Q4 and up from $1.88B in last year’s Q1.

Provision for credit losses grew to $219M from $156M in the prior quarter and $155M a year ago.

PNC's (NYSE:PNC) Q1 total noninterest expense of $3.39B fell 3% Q/Q and increased 2% Y/Y.

Average loans decreased to $316.6B from $319.1B in the previous quarter. Average deposits fell to $420.6B from $425.3B in Q4.

Conference call at 10:00 AM ET.

Earlier, PNC Financial GAAP EPS of $3.51 beats by $0.13, revenue of $5.45B misses by $30M

data.title: Futures little changed as traders continue to play 'guess-the-tariffs'
data.description:
Stock index futures were struggling for direction Tuesday after a modest gain to start the week.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND), S&P futures (SPX) and Dow futures (INDU) were down around -0.1%.

"With the latest advance for equities, that now means that the S&P 500 has recovered to -4.67% beneath its level at the tariff announcement, and -12.01% beneath its mid-February peak," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said. "That’s clearly a significant decline, but it’s still some distance from a normal recession decline, or even some of the larger non-recession bear markets of recent years like 2022, which saw a -25% peak-to-trough fall. So it’s clear that investors aren’t convinced that a recession is inevitable just yet."

Investors were still trying to gauge what kinds of tariff landscape the global economy will face. 

President Donald Trump has hinted at a reprieve for the 25% tariffs on automobile and auto part imports, while the administration has begun investigating the impact of pharmaceutical and semiconductor imports on national security, likely setting the stage for tariffs on the sectors.

Trump "signaled a possible retreat from auto tariffs to allow companies to relocate production to the US," UBS chief economist Paul Donovan said. "There are some challenges to this thinking. Relocation would not be quick. Relocation would add costs (unnecessarily duplicating factories). Relocation would require long-term investment in an erratic policy environment."

Treasury yields were slightly higher after recent declines. The 10-year yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.38%.

On the economic calendar, the April Empire State Manufacturing Index arrives before the bell. The forecast is for a rise to -12.80 from -20.

"We think a sharp hit to business confidence from President Trump’s shock tariff announcement means the headline index will follow the 25.7-point plunge in March with a further small decline in April," Pantheon Macro said. "We look for a dip to minus 25, from minus 20."

At the same time, March import/export price numbers hit. Both are expected to have risen 0.1% on the month.

"US March import and export price data does not show the inflation from tariffs (that is applied after the import prices are calculated)," Donovan said. "Monitoring the trends in import price data should confirm that the tariff tax burden is paid by the US, as is generally the case."

data.title: Shell signs contract with Bulgaria for Black Sea oil and gas exploration
data.description:
Shell (NYSE:SHEL) has signed a contract with Bulgaria's government for the rights to explore for oil and gas in the Black Sea, the country's energy minister said Tuesday.

The contract will allow Shell (NYSE:SHEL) to explore 4,000 square metres in the Block 1-26 Khan Tervel field.

Bulgaria, which had been totally dependent on Russian gas until 2022, has been seeking to diversify its gas supplies and find cheaper sources; OMV (OTCPK:OMVJF) and NewMed Energy (OTCPK:DKDRF) recently signed a contract with the government for oil and gas exploration in another field off the Black Sea coast.

"We believe we can bring the best of our technology and our expertise and a track record to unlock the potential offshore Black Sea in Bulgaria," Shell (SHEL) executive VP of exploration, strategy and portfolio Eugene Okpere told Reuters.

data.title: ParaZero launches autonomous parachute recovery system, SafeAir M4
data.description:
• ParaZero Technologies (NASDAQ:PRZO) announced on Tuesday the launch of SafeAir M4, its next-generation autonomous parachute recovery system now fully compatible with the DJI Matrice 4 series. 
  • The SafeAir M4 represents the most advanced version of ParaZero’s SafeAir system, incorporating aircraft-grade technology, a newly developed airbag-based deployment mechanism, plug-and-play design, and multi-layered compliance with both U.S. and European aviation regulations.

data.title: Aurora Innovation lands bull rating from Needham ahead of autonomous trucking breakthroughs
data.description:
Aurora Innovation (NASDAQ:AUR) broke higher in early trading on Tuesday after Needham initiated coverage with a Buy rating on the autonomous driving tech stock.

Analyst Chris Pierce said Aurora Innovation (NASDAQ:AUR) is an industry leader in autonomous mobility, noting the company looks set to leverage the Aurora Driver platform and industry partnerships to create the market for autonomous trucking on U.S. highways.

Crucially, Aurora Innovation (AUR) is seen winning share in a $1 trillion market and generating revenue on a per-mile driven basis, while also solving for industry pain points and allowing for faster, cheaper transportation of goods.

"We see a long runway of durable growth, with AUR mapping and adding new routes while scaling trucks on the road upfitted with the Aurora Driver platform leaning on OEM and Tier 1 supplier partnerships to grow autonomous miles driven," wrote Pierce.

Needham's price target on AUR of $10 is 10X the 2031 adjusted EBITDA estimate discounted back. The PT was noted to be underwritten by AUR winning a conservative share of Class 8 trucks on the road, but driving a higher share of Class 8 truck miles traveled.

Shares of Aurora Innovation (AUR) rose 2.7% in premarket action to $6.19 vs. the 52-week range of $2.10 to $10.77.

data.title: Vince Holding names Yuji Okumura CFO
data.description:
• Vince Holding (NYSE:VNCE) said on Tuesday that Yuji Okumura, the company's interim Chief Financial Officer since March 2025 and Controller since September 2020, has been named Chief Financial Officer, effective April 14, 2025.
  • Okumura has nearly two decades of experience in accounting and financial reporting. He has served as the company’s Vice President, Controller since 2020 after originally joining the organization in 2018 as its Director of Financial Reporting. 
  • Prior to Vince Holding, Okumura worked for over 11 years in public accounting at KPMG US. 
  • Vince Holding (NYSE:VNCE) shares were up 5.59% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Colliers to acquire Canadian commercial real estate services platform, Triovest
data.description:
• Colliers (NASDAQ:CIGI) said on Tuesday that it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Triovest, a Canadian commercial real estate services platform, from its owner, Coril Holdings.
  • After closing, Triovest will rebrand as Colliers, merging its operations and capabilities to solidify Colliers’ position as Canada’s largest commercial real estate services firm. 
  • The combined Canadian operations will employ more than 3,000 professionals, manage more than 95 million square feet of commercial real estate, and oversee more than $15 billion in projects under development. Terms of the transaction were not disclosed.
  • Colliers (NASDAQ:CIGI) shares were down 0.60% in extended trading.

data.title: Lazard announces strategic expansion into the UAE
data.description:
Financial advisory and asset management firm Lazard (NYSE:LAZ) announced on Tuesday a strategic expansion into the United Arab Emirates, with Abu Dhabi becoming the financial advisory main office for the country.

Hussain Altajir is appointed CEO of Lazard Financial Advisory in the UAE.

data.title: Funko plans to expand to Southeast Asia with a store in the Philippines
data.description:
Funko (FNK) announced on Tuesday it will open its first Southeast Asian Funko-licensed store in the Philippines through a partnership with local retailer Funtastik Enterprises Corp. The store is set to debut in June 2025 at SM Mall of Asia, one of the region’s largest shopping destinations. 

The store will feature exclusive product releases and interactive zones, celebrating a wide range of entertainment, sports, and music franchises, as well as the growing world of anime. Shoppers can expect to find a variety of collectibles, including the iconic Funko Pop! line, Bitty Pop!, Loungefly, and Manila-exclusive apparel.

The toy company said the expansion is a significant step in its international growth strategy, reflecting the commitment to bringing its brand experience closer to its most passionate communities. The Philippines was chosen by Funko (FNK) due to its vibrant pop culture scene, characterized by thriving comic conventions, strong community engagement, and a rapidly expanding e-commerce ecosystem. 

Notably, Filipino fans are among the world's most engaged Funko (NASDAQ:FNKO) collectors, making the market a strategic choice for further local engagement and fanbase expansion. Funko (NASDAQ:FNKO) CEO Cynthia Williams emphasized that the Philippines is one of the company’s strongest-performing markets in Asia and that the new store represents a direct investment in its dedicated fan community. 

With the launch, Funko (FNKO) said it aims to accelerate its global reach and shape the next era of fan culture in Asia and beyond, leveraging best-in-class retail and licensing partnerships to inspire connection, self-expression, and fun among fans worldwide.

Shares of Funk (FNKO) moved 0.5% higher in premarket trading to $4.27 vs. the 52-week range of $4.18 to $14.65.

data.title: Church & Dwight is called a consumer staples standout by BofA
data.description:
Church & Dwight Co. (NYSE:CHD) gained in early trading on Tuesday after Bank of America upgraded the household products stocks to a Buy rating from Neutral and called it a standout in the consumer staples sector.

The positive view is that Church & Dwight (NYSE:CHD) is poised to benefit from a challenging consumer backdrop and potential trade-down with its value portfolio.

The firm also sees a compelling risk/reward setup due to improving volumes with consistent market share gains, limited private label risk to the value portfolio, potential for tuck-in M&A, and a history of outperformance in economic downturns.  

Analyst Anna Lizzul highlighted CHD's exposure to value products while continuing to elevate the premium portfolio through innovation

"We view CHD’s sales portfolio mix with 36% value and 64% premium, and ~82% US sales, as well positioned in a recessionary environment. CHD’s portfolio has a combination of improving volume trends and market share gains with low risk to private label, as only five of CHD’s 18 categories have private label exposure."

Bank of America assigned a price objective of $30 to CHD based on a 2026 P/E ratio of 30X. The premium valuation was said to be an appropriate premium for consistent volume growth supported by innovation andlow risk to private label, with minimal risk to tariff impact. 

Shares of Church & Dwight Co. (CHD) were up 1.0% in premarket action to $106.58 vs. the 52-week range of $96.35 to $116.46.
